003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.http.header;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
021
022import java.util.function.*;
023
024/**
025 * Represents an HTTP <c>Content-Range</c> header.
026 *
027 * <p>
028 * Where in a full body message this partial message belongs.
029 *
030 * <p>
031 * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
032 * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
033 * It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the <b>next major</b> Juneau release
034 * (and possibly earlier).
035 *
036 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
037 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/NextGenRestClient">juneau-ng REST client</a>
038 * </ul>
039 *
040 * @since 9.2.1
041 */
042public class ContentRange extends HttpStringHeader {
043
044   /** The header name */
045   public static final String NAME = "Content-Range";
046
047   /**
048    * Constructor with an eager value.
049    *
050    * @param value The header value. May be <jk>null</jk>.
051    */
052   public ContentRange(String value) {
053      super(NAME, value);
054   }
055
056   /**
057    * Constructor with a lazy value supplier.
058    *
059    * @param valueSupplier Supplier for the header value. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
060    */
061   public ContentRange(Supplier<String> valueSupplier) {
062      super(NAME, valueSupplier);
063   }
064
065   /**
066    * Static factory method with an eager value.
067    *
068    * @param value The header value. May be <jk>null</jk>.
069    * @return A new instance. Never <jk>null</jk>.
070    */
071   public static ContentRange of(String value) {
072      return new ContentRange(value);
073   }
074
075   /**
076    * Static factory method with a lazy value supplier.
077    *
078    * @param valueSupplier Supplier for the header value. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
079    * @return A new instance. Never <jk>null</jk>.
080    */
081   public static ContentRange of(Supplier<String> valueSupplier) {
082      return new ContentRange(valueSupplier);
083   }
084
085   /**
086    * Creates a new {@link Builder} defaulting to the <c>bytes</c> range unit.
087    *
088    * @return A new builder. Never <jk>null</jk>.
089    */
090   public static Builder create() {
091      return new Builder();
092   }
093
094   //------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
095   // Inner types
096   //------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
097
098   /**
099    * Fluent builder for assembling an HTTP <c>Content-Range</c> header value.
100    *
101    * <p>
102    * Composes the three fields defined by
103    * <a class='doclink' href='https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7233#section-4.2'>RFC 7233 §4.2</a> — the range unit
104    * (default <c>bytes</c>), the resolved <c>first-last</c> byte range (or <c>*</c> for an unsatisfied range), and the
105    * <c>complete-length</c> (or <c>*</c> when unknown) — into forms such as <c>bytes 0-1023/2048</c>,
106    * <c>bytes 0-1023/&#42;</c>, and <c>bytes &#42;/2048</c>.
107    *
108    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
109    * <p class='bjava'>
110    *    String <jv>value</jv> = ContentRange.<jsm>create</jsm>()
111    *       .range(0, 1023)
112    *       .length(2048)
113    *       .build();
114    *    <jc>// =&gt; "bytes 0-1023/2048"</jc>
115    * </p>
116    *
117    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
118    *    <li class='jc'>{@link ContentRange}
119    *    <li class='extlink'><a class='doclink' href='https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7233'>RFC 7233 - HTTP/1.1 Range Requests</a>
120    * </ul>
121    *
122    * @since 10.0.0
123    */
124   public static class Builder {
125
126      private String unit = "bytes";
127      private Long start;
128      private Long end;
129      private boolean rangeUnsatisfied;
130      private Long completeLength;
131      private boolean lengthUnknown;
132
133      /**
134       * Sets the range unit (default <c>bytes</c>).
135       *
136       * @param value The range unit. Must not be <jk>null</jk> or blank.
137       * @return This object.
138       * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code value} is <jk>null</jk> or blank.
139       */
140      public Builder unit(String value) {
141         assertArgNotNull("value", value);
142         var v = value.trim();
143         if (v.isEmpty())
144            throw illegalArg("range unit must not be blank");
145         unit = v;
146         return this;
147      }
148
149      /**
150       * Sets the resolved <c>first-last</c> byte range.
151       *
152       * <p>
153       * Clears any previously-set {@link #unsatisfiedRange() unsatisfied} marker.
154       *
155       * @param startValue The first byte position (inclusive). Must be non-negative.
156       * @param endValue The last byte position (inclusive). Must be greater than or equal to {@code startValue}.
157       * @return This object.
158       * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the positions are negative or out of order.
159       */
160      public Builder range(long startValue, long endValue) {
161         if (startValue < 0)
162            throw illegalArg("range start must be non-negative: {0}", startValue);
163         if (endValue < startValue)
164            throw illegalArg("range end {0} must be >= start {1}", endValue, startValue);
165         start = startValue;
166         end = endValue;
167         rangeUnsatisfied = false;
168         return this;
169      }
170
171      /**
172       * Marks the range portion as unsatisfied, rendering it as <c>*</c> (used with a known complete length).
173       *
174       * @return This object.
175       */
176      public Builder unsatisfiedRange() {
177         rangeUnsatisfied = true;
178         start = null;
179         end = null;
180         return this;
181      }
182
183      /**
184       * Sets the <c>complete-length</c> (the total size of the representation).
185       *
186       * <p>
187       * Clears any previously-set {@link #unknownLength() unknown-length} marker.
188       *
189       * @param value The complete length in units. Must be non-negative.
190       * @return This object.
191       * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code value} is negative.
192       */
193      public Builder length(long value) {
194         if (value < 0)
195            throw illegalArg("complete-length must be non-negative: {0}", value);
196         completeLength = value;
197         lengthUnknown = false;
198         return this;
199      }
200
201      /**
202       * Marks the complete length as unknown, rendering it as <c>*</c>.
203       *
204       * @return This object.
205       */
206      public Builder unknownLength() {
207         lengthUnknown = true;
208         completeLength = null;
209         return this;
210      }
211
212      /**
213       * Builds the rendered <c>Content-Range</c> header value.
214       *
215       * @return The header value. Never <jk>null</jk>.
216       */
217      public String build() {
218         var rangePart = rangeUnsatisfied || start == null ? "*" : start + "-" + end;
219         var lengthPart = lengthUnknown || completeLength == null ? "*" : completeLength.toString();
220         return unit + " " + rangePart + "/" + lengthPart;
221      }
222
223      /**
224       * Builds a {@link ContentRange} header bean directly from this builder.
225       *
226       * @return A {@link ContentRange} carrying {@link #build()}. Never <jk>null</jk>.
227       */
228      public ContentRange toHeader() {
229         return ContentRange.of(build());
230      }
231
232      @Override
233      public String toString() {
234         return build();
235      }
236   }
237}
